raw_response:
{
  "decision": "APPROVE_WITH_CONDITIONS",
  "rationale": "The GenAI chatbot addresses a critical degradation in support service levels (response time up from 3 to 11 hours) and aligns with the need to handle a 45% ticket volume increase. The CTO’s projected 35% ticket deflection could generate sufficient cost savings to meet the CFO’s 12‑month payback target, provided the reduction materialises. However, the absence of AI governance, data‑privacy safeguards, and clear workforce transition plans introduces significant compliance and people‑risk that must be mitigated before full rollout.",
  "financial_considerations": [
    "Up‑front implementation cost: $250,000.",
    "Ongoing operating cost: $30,000 per month ($360,000 per year).",
    "Total first‑year cash outlay: $610,000.",
    "Projected savings: 35% ticket deflection could reduce agent workload equivalent to ~42 agents; at an average fully‑loaded cost of $50,000 per agent, annual savings ≈ $2.1 M.",
    "Break‑even point: Expected within 6‑9 months if deflection targets are met.",
    "Sensitivity: If deflection falls below 20%, payback extends beyond 12 months; ROI becomes marginal."
  ],
  "operational_considerations": [
    "Integration with existing ticketing platform (APIs, data flow, fallback routing).",
    "Pilot phase limited to FAQ‑heavy categories (estimated 30% of tickets) for 3 months.",
    "Performance monitoring: deflection rate, accuracy of draft responses, escalation latency.",
    "Rollback procedures to human agents for any chatbot failure or compliance breach.",
    "Scalability: infrastructure must support peak ticket volumes without degrading response times."
  ],
  "people_impact": [
    "Potential reduction in repetitive first‑level tasks; agents can focus on complex cases.",
    "Risk of perceived job loss; requires transparent communication and reskilling program.",
    "Proposed redeployment plan: up‑skill 30% of agents for advanced support, quality assurance, and chatbot supervision roles.",
    "Change‑management timeline: 4‑week training on chatbot hand‑off processes and draft‑response editing."
  ],
  "compliance_risks": [
    "Customer tickets contain personally identifiable information (PII) – risk of unlawful processing.",
    "No current AI governance framework; exposure to regulatory scrutiny (GDPR, CCPA, industry‑specific rules).",
    "Data residency and retention policies must be enforced for chatbot logs.",
    "Need for a Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) before production deployment."
  ],
  "conditions_for_approval": [
    "Develop and adopt an AI governance policy covering model validation, bias monitoring, and audit trails before go‑live.",
    "Conduct a DPIA and implement data‑masking or redaction for any PII processed by the chatbot.",
    "Execute a 3‑month pilot covering ≤30% of ticket volume; proceed to full rollout only if deflection ≥20% and accuracy ≥90% on pilot metrics.",
    "Establish a workforce transition plan with at least 8 weeks of up‑skilling for affected agents and a clear communication strategy.",
    "Set quarterly financial reviews to track cost savings against the $610k outlay; halt further investment if ROI targets are not on track.",
    "Implement a governance oversight committee (CIO, CISO, Compliance, HR) to review chatbot performance, compliance logs, and incident reports."
  ],
  "final_recommendation": "Approve the GenAI chatbot project contingent on the above conditions. This balanced approach enables the company to address deteriorating support metrics and achieve the desired ROI while safeguarding compliance, governance, and employee morale."
}

parsed_output.rationale: The GenAI chatbot addresses a critical degradation in support service levels (response time up from 3 to 11 hours) and aligns with the need to handle a 45% ticket volume increase. The CTO’s projected 35% ticket deflection could generate sufficient cost savings to meet the CFO’s 12‑month payback target, provided the reduction materialises. However, the absence of AI governance, data‑privacy safeguards, and clear workforce transition plans introduces significant compliance and people‑risk that must be mitigated before full rollout.
financial_considerations: Ongoing operating cost: $30,000 per month ($360,000 per year).
Projected savings: 35% ticket deflection could reduce agent workload equivalent to ~42 agents; at an average fully‑loaded cost of $50,000 per agent, annual savings ≈ $2.1 M.
Break‑even point: Expected within 6‑9 months if deflection targets are met.
Sensitivity: If deflection falls below 20%, payback extends beyond 12 months; ROI becomes marginal.
operational_considerations: Integration with existing ticketing platform (APIs, data flow, fallback routing).
Pilot phase limited to FAQ‑heavy categories (estimated 30% of tickets) for 3 months.
Performance monitoring: deflection rate, accuracy of draft responses, escalation latency.
Rollback procedures to human agents for any chatbot failure or compliance breach.
Scalability: infrastructure must support peak ticket volumes without degrading response times.
people_impact: Potential reduction in repetitive first‑level tasks; agents can focus on complex cases.
Risk of perceived job loss; requires transparent communication and reskilling program.
Proposed redeployment plan: up‑skill 30% of agents for advanced support, quality assurance, and chatbot supervision roles.
Change‑management timeline: 4‑week training on chatbot hand‑off processes and draft‑response editing.
compliance_risks: Customer tickets contain personally identifiable information (PII) – risk of unlawful processing.
No current AI governance framework; exposure to regulatory scrutiny (GDPR, CCPA, industry‑specific rules).
Data residency and retention policies must be enforced for chatbot logs.
Need for a Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) before production deployment.
conditions_for_approval: Develop and adopt an AI governance policy covering model validation, bias monitoring, and audit trails before go‑live.
Conduct a DPIA and implement data‑masking or redaction for any PII processed by the chatbot.
Execute a 3‑month pilot covering ≤30% of ticket volume; proceed to full rollout only if deflection ≥20% and accuracy ≥90% on pilot metrics.
Establish a workforce transition plan with at least 8 weeks of up‑skilling for affected agents and a clear communication strategy.
Set quarterly financial reviews to track cost savings against the $610k outlay; halt further investment if ROI targets are not on track.
Implement a governance oversight committee (CIO, CISO, Compliance, HR) to review chatbot performance, compliance logs, and incident reports.
parsed_output.final_recommendation: Approve the GenAI chatbot project contingent on the above conditions. This balanced approach enables the company to address deteriorating support metrics and achieve the desired ROI while safeguarding compliance, governance, and employee morale.
